# tenzir_service_token (Resource)

An organization-level service token: a static machine credential (`tzst_...`) that acts inside its organization as a synthetic member with the configured role, e.g. as the `service_token` credential of this provider. Requires org-admin credentials.

The platform has no update operation for service tokens, so changing any attribute replaces (revokes and re-creates) the token. Expired or revoked tokens are planned for re-creation on the next refresh.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization_id` (String) Identifier of the organization the token belongs to.

### Optional

- `expires_in_seconds` (Number) Lifetime of the token in seconds, counted from its creation. Unset means the token never expires. Only used at creation time; the platform does not return it, so it stays unset for imported tokens.
- `label` (String) Human-readable label of the token.
- `role` (String) Organization role of the token's service identity, `member` or `admin`. Defaults to `member`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the token (not the secret).
- `token` (String, Sensitive) The plaintext token secret (`tzst_...`). The platform reveals it exactly once at creation time and only persists its hash, so it stays unset for imported tokens. Stored in the Terraform state; treat the state as sensitive.
